claims.text: The variant c.1935C>A (p.D645E) is listed in Table 3 for Patient 1 (heterozygous, maternal origin, classified as pathogenic) in compound heterozygosity with c.1669A>T (p.I557F, paternal, pathogenic) in exon 12.

citations.quote:
1 | exon12 | c.1669A > T | p.I557F | het | paternal | pathogenic |
| | exon14 | c.1935C > A | p.D645E | het | maternal | pathogenic |

claims.text: Patient 8 carries c.1935C>A (p.D645E) in homozygous form (parental origin), classified as pathogenic.

citations.quote: 8 | exon14 | c.1935C > A | p.D645E | hom | parental | pathogenic |

claims.text: The variant c.1935C>A is noted to account for 80% of mutant alleles in Thai patients with infantile-onset Pompe disease, making it the most common mutation in the Thai population.

citations.quote: In Thai patients, Amarinthnukrowh et al. reported that mutation c.1935C > A accounted for 80% of the mutant alleles and is the most common mutation in the Thai population (7).

claims.text: The paper references p.D645E (c.1935C>A) as the most common mutation in southern Chinese patients, accounting for 20–25% of total mutant alleles.

citations.quote: The most common mutation in patients from southern China was c.1C > A (p.Asp 645Glu), accounting for 20%–25% of the total mutant alleles (20).

claims.text: Both patients with this variant (Patients 1 and 8) had infantile-onset Pompe disease and died at a median age of 24.80 months due to cardiorespiratory failure; all 15 patients in the cohort presented with myasthenia symptoms and severe hypertrophic cardiomyopathy.

citations.quote: Fifteen patients died at a median age of 24.80 months due to cardiorespiratory failure (range 3–120 months). Myasthenia symptoms and severe hypertrophic cardiomyopathy were universally present (15/15 = 100%).
